perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement supersedes all prior written or oral negotiations, commitments, agreement and understandings between the Parties relating to confidentiality and constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to confidentiality. The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part, except by a writing signed by the Parties’ authorised representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement supersedes all prior written or oral negotiations, commitments, agreement and understandings between the Parties relating to confidentiality and constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to confidentiality. The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part, except by a verbal agreement from both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates written amendments. The modified clause allows verbal amendments. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity regarding how the contract can be modified and enforced. The introduction of verbal agreement, directly contradicts the existing requirement of a 'writing signed by the Parties' authorized representatives.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. This Agreement supersedes all prior written or oral negotiations, commitments, agreement and understandings between the Parties relating to confidentiality and constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to confidentiality. The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part, except by a verbal agreement from both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that modifications to the agreement must be in writing and signed by authorized representatives. The changed text now allows for verbal agreements to modify the contract, directly contradicting the original requirement of a written and signed amendment. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because both clauses (the changed text and the contradicted text, which are the same in this case) cannot be simultaneously true and enforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The Parties will comply with all appli cable laws in the use of the Confidential Information and performance of this Agreement, including without limitation the EU, UK and U.S. export control and trading sanctions rules and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The Parties will comply with all applicable laws in the use of the Confidential Information and performance of this Agreement, except for U.S. export control and trading sanctions rules and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction. Initially, the parties agree to comply with all applicable laws including U.S. export control laws. The modified version explicitly excludes U.S. export control laws. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding legal compliance and introduces uncertainty. The clause now explicitly state they will *not* follow those regulation, which it previously agreed to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The Parties will comply with all applicable laws in the use of the Confidential Information and performance of this Agreement, except for U.S. export control and trading sanctions rules and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text states that the parties will comply with all applicable laws, including U.S. export control laws. The changed text explicitly states that the parties are not required to comply with U.S. export control laws, which contradicts the initial agreement. The contradiction text is identical to the changed text, implying that the contradictory statement exists in the contract, hence resulting in a contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The Parties agree that breach of this Agreement may result in a Party suffering irreparable harm from the unauthorised use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and may not have an adequate remedy in money or damage s. Accordingly, the Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief, and any other r elief permitted by applicable law, from any court of competent jurisdiction to enforce its rights under this agreement. Failure or delay by a Party to enforce any right, power, or privilege created in this Agreement will not operate as an implied waiver th ereof, nor will any single or partial enforcement preclude any other or further enforcement of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The Parties agree that breach of this Agreement may result in a Party suffering irreparable harm from the unauthorised use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and may not have an adequate remedy in money or damage s. Accordingly, the Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief, or monetary damages, from any court of competent jurisdiction to enforce its rights under this agreement. Failure or delay by a Party to enforce any right, power, or privilege created in this Agreement will operate as an implied waiver thereof, nor will any single or partial enforcement preclude any other or further enforcement of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause stated that irreparable harm 'may not have an adequate remedy in money or damages', but then allows for 'any other relief permitted by applicable law'. The modified version states that the parties will be entitled to 'injunctive relief, or monetary damages'. This creates a direct in-text contradiction. Also, where before a failure to enforce wasn't a waiver, now it's waiver.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. The Parties agree that breach of this Agreement may result in a Party suffering irreparable harm from the unauthorised use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and may not have an adequate remedy in money or damage s. Accordingly, the Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief, or monetary damages, from any court of competent jurisdiction to enforce its rights under this agreement. Failure or delay by a Party to enforce any right, power, or privilege created in this Agreement will operate as an implied waiver thereof, nor will any single or partial enforcement preclude any other or further enforcement of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that breach 'may not have an adequate remedy in money or damages' while the changed text says the parties will be entitled to 'injunctive relief, or monetary damages'. This introduces a contradiction because the changed text now allows for monetary damages where the original text suggested they might not be an adequate remedy. Furthermore, the original text stated failure to enforce wasn't a waiver, and now the changed text states it is a waiver, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradictory text is in the same location as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: DEFINITIONS “Confidential Information” m eans all confidential and proprietary information disclosed to or obtained by the Receiving Party or its Representatives from the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, including without limitation, information relating to: proprietary technology, produc ts, proprietary plans, services, computer programs and processes; data and source code; any inventions and associated information, manuals, plans, drawings, blueprints or schematics; information relating to CWF0 001 Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement C.W. Fletcher & Sons Ltd employees and customers of the Disclosing Party; promotional and marketing information and opportunities; financial information; trade secrets; any and all information that a Receiving Party may obtain on a walk -through examination of the Disclosing Party’s and/or its clients’ premises; the existence of discussions between the Parties regarding the Purpose, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status of such discussions); and all information, of any nature whatsoever, disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the R eceiving Party in connection with the Purpose, whether before or after the date of this Agreement. In each case such information may be contained in tangible materials including, but not limited to writings, drawings, models, prototypes, test specimens, pr oduction units, data specifications, reports, analysis, methods, compilations and computer programs, or may be in the nature of verbally or visually communicated intellectual knowledge.
perturbation.changed_text: DEFINITIONS “Confidential Information” m eans all confidential and proprietary information disclosed to or obtained by the Receiving Party or its Representatives from the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, including without limitation, information relating to: proprietary technology, produc ts, proprietary plans, services, computer programs and processes; data and source code; any inventions and associated information, manuals, plans, drawings, blueprints or schematics; information relating to employees and customers of the Disclosing Party; promotional and marketing information and opportunities; financial information; trade secrets; any and all information that a Receiving Party may obtain on a walk -through examination of the Disclosing Party’s and/or its clients’ premises; the existence of discussions between the Parties regarding the Purpose, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status of such discussions); and all information, of any nature whatsoever, disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the R eceiving Party in connection with the Purpose, whether before or after the date of this Agreement. In each case such information may be contained in tangible materials including, but not limited to writings, drawings, models, prototypes, test specimens, pr oduction units, data specifications, reports, analysis, methods, compilations and computer programs, or may be in the nature of verbally or visually communicated intellectual knowledge. However, this definition EXCLUDES any information readily available in the public domain.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause explicitly excludes information already in the public domain from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. However, paragraph 2 already addresses public domain information. Now, even if public domain information breaches the agreement, the amended definition claims it isn't 'confidential information' leading to confusion of enforcing the rules and the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Paragraph 1. will not apply to any Con fidential Information which: - A. is in or comes into the public domain otherwise than by a breach of this Agreement or B. the Receiving Party has in its possessions without restriction on disclosure prior to the receipt from the Disclosing Party, or C. The Receivin g Party lawfully receives from a bona -fide third party without restriction on disclosure, or D. Is developed independently by the Receiving Party without any portion of the development having been based on the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly. The changed text adds an exclusion for information 'readily available in the public domain'. The contradicted text in paragraph 2 already addresses circumstances under which confidential information is or comes into the public domain (specifically, 2.A, 'is in or comes into the public domain otherwise than by a breach of this Agreement'). By excluding all public domain information from the definition, the changed text creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. Now, if information becomes public due to a breach of the agreement, the contradictory text states it's not considered confidential information, which directly conflicts with section 2.A. Therefore, this is a clear in-text contradiction because the amended definition conflicts with the obligations in section 2.A, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for any purpose it deems necessary. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the Receiving Party to use the information for evaluating or preparing a quotation. The modified version states the Receiving Party can use the information for 'any purpose it deems necessary'. The in-text contradiction, introduces ambiguity regarding permissible uses of the Confidential Information, directly contradicting the limitation initially outlined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for any purpose it deems necessary. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the Receiving Party's use of confidential information to 'evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of the Purpose.' The changed text replaces this with 'any purpose it deems necessary.' This directly contradicts the initial limitation on use, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, which now contains conflicting statements about the permissible uses of confidential information within the same section (Clause A).
